activationPolicy: Registry presence never implies skill activation.

skills.name: riss-governance
skills.description: User-facing governance entrypoint for scoped repo governance, source-of-truth checks, narrow agent/tool routing, safety gates, and completion verification.
triggerCases: Task involves AI Toolkit, RISS, Supabase/backend/security/release governance, source-of-truth checks, custom agent routing, or completion verification.
Plain-language request needs professional engineering routing.
negativeTriggerCases: Unrelated projects where user did not request this governance layer.
Simple one-off questions where repo governance does not materially help.
Requests to install or activate every tool without review.
conflicts: Do not duplicate Superpowers or Codex plugins.
Do not silently fall back from native agent to compiled instructions for high-risk work.

skills.name: riss-agent-governance
skills.description: Planned internal helper skill for agent inventory, native visibility, compiled fallback status, routing, scorecards, and handoff governance.
triggerCases: Future internal routing needs agent selection or fallback status.
negativeTriggerCases: Phase 10A/10B implementation before the registry contract is proven.
conflicts: Must not become a large user-facing governance skill.

skills.name: riss-skill-governance
skills.description: Planned internal helper skill for skill inventory, trigger conditions, conflict resolution, missing-capability discovery, and skill safety.
triggerCases: Future internal routing needs skill selection, trigger checks, or missing capability discovery.
negativeTriggerCases: Installing, activating, cloning, or copying external skills.
conflicts: Must not install or activate external skills.
Must not copy raw external SKILL.md content.
